content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO COUNTY                    SHERIFF'S DEPARTMENT

Glen Craig
Sheriff

September 7, 1990


John Rothman
California Fair Political
  Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear John:

Thank you very much for taking the time to explain Regulation
18538 and its intent to cover candidates in the November election.

I am pleased that you have indicated the Commission never intended
to restrict participation in ballot issues by those candidates who
were either unopposed in the June election, or won outright in
non-partisan races and, therefore, do not appear on the November
ballot.

I would appreciate very much a written confirmation of our
conversation this date, which will provide that interpretation to
me.  As I indicated, Supervisor Illa Collin, District Attorney
Steve White, Auditor-Controller Nancy Wolford, and I will all be
actively involved in the Charter Amendment providing for the
consolidation of Sacramento City and County government.  I am also
involved with two other state-wide propositions.

Since I have no campaign, and I do not appear on the ballot, your
regulation without this interpretation would be a serious
imposition on my ability to participate in governmental affairs.

Thank you for your consideration.  I would appreciate a prompt
response.

Very truly yours,

Glen Craig
GLEN CRAIG, Sheriff

GC/sgb

REFER ALL CORRESPONDENCE TO: GLEN CRAIG, SHERIFF • P.O. BOX 988 • SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95812-0988        26

sections.question_synthetic: Can candidates who were unopposed in the June election or won outright in non-partisan races participate in ballot issue campaigns in the November election without violating Regulation 18538?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The sender (Sheriff Craig) is requesting written confirmation of a prior verbal conversation with FPPC staff member John Rothman regarding Regulation 18538 interpretation. No formal question or conclusion sections present in this letter format. Document appears to be the citizen's correspondence to FPPC, not FPPC's advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can candidates who were unopposed in the June election or won outright in non-partisan races participate in ballot issue campaigns in the November election without violating Regulation 18538?

embedding.summary: Sacramento County Sheriff Glen Craig requests written confirmation from the FPPC that Regulation 18538 does not restrict candidates who are unopposed or won in June elections from participating in ballot issue campaigns in November, citing his involvement in Charter Amendment and state-wide proposition campaigns.
